title: Fix Cairo assertion crash in GTK4 SKDrawingArea
body:
## Problem

`SKDrawingArea` in `SkiaSharp.Views.Gtk4` can crash with a Cairo assertion failure (SIGABRT) during repeated draw cycles.

### Root Cause

The `OnDrawFunc` method writes pixel data via Skia into a shared `Cairo.ImageSurface` buffer, then calls `pix.MarkDirty()` to tell Cairo the pixels have changed. However, it was **missing** a `pix.Flush()` call before `MarkDirty()`.

Cairo internally snapshots surfaces during `SetSourceSurface`/`Paint` operations. When `MarkDirty()` is called while a snapshot still holds a reference to the underlying data, Cairo's snapshot-COW (copy-on-write) mechanism triggers an assertion or produces corrupted rendering.

### The Fix

Add `pix.Flush()` immediately before `pix.MarkDirty()`. This follows the [documented Cairo pattern](https://www.cairographics.org/manual/cairo-cairo-surface-t.html#cairo-surface-mark-dirty) for external pixel modification:

```
cairo_surface_flush(surface);     // complete any pending reads from snapshots
// ... modify pixel data externally (via Skia) ...
cairo_surface_mark_dirty(surface); // notify Cairo that pixels changed
```

### Before (crashes)
```csharp
surface.Canvas.Flush();
pix.MarkDirty();  // ❌ snapshot may still reference the data
```

### After (correct)
```csharp
surface.Canvas.Flush();
pix.Flush();      // ✅ release snapshot references first
pix.MarkDirty();
```

### How to Reproduce

The crash occurs on repeated redraws of an `SKDrawingArea` in a GTK4 application, particularly noticeable when the draw function is called in rapid succession (e.g., animations or interactive drawing). The SIGABRT comes from Cairo's internal `_cairo_surface_begin_modification` assertion.

### Notes

- This is a one-line fix with no behavioral change other than preventing the crash
- The GTK3 `SKDrawingArea` does not have this issue because it uses a different drawing path (`ExposeEvent` → `Pixbuf`)
- Discovered while testing the GTK4 sample application's drawing page
